source: GoodRx, AARP, CMS drug pricing data, NeedyMeds
dataAgeNote: Drug prices change frequently. RICHY SHOULD WEB SEARCH for current GoodRx prices. This data provides general savings strategies and program information.

goodrx.name: GoodRx
goodrx.cost: Free (premium version $9.99/month for additional family savings)
goodrx.howItWorks: Shows prices at every pharmacy near you and provides coupons. Average savings: 60-80% on generic drugs.
goodrx.example: Generic Lipitor (atorvastatin): Retail $80-150/month → GoodRx price $4-12/month at Costco or Kroger
goodrx.tip: Always check GoodRx BEFORE using insurance — sometimes the GoodRx price is lower than your insurance copay.

costPlus.name: Mark Cuban Cost Plus Drugs
costPlus.howItWorks: Online pharmacy selling generics at cost + 15% markup + $5 shipping + $3 pharmacy fee. Radically transparent pricing.
costPlus.example: Generic Crestor (rosuvastatin): Typical retail $50-200/month → Cost Plus $4-8/month
costPlus.tip: Best for maintenance medications you take monthly. Order 90-day supplies to minimize shipping costs.

rxAssist.name: RxAssist / Patient Assistance Programs
rxAssist.howItWorks: Database of pharmaceutical company programs that provide free or low-cost medications to qualifying patients.
rxAssist.eligibility: Usually income-based. Many programs cover people making up to 400% of federal poverty level.
rxAssist.tip: If you're on expensive brand-name drugs, check if the manufacturer has a patient assistance program. Many people qualify and don't know.

medicare_extra_help.name: Medicare Extra Help (Low-Income Subsidy)
medicare_extra_help.howItWorks: Helps Medicare beneficiaries pay for prescriptions. Savings up to $5,000/year.
medicare_extra_help.eligibility: Income below $22,590 (single) or $30,660 (married) and limited resources
medicare_extra_help.tip: Over 2 million eligible people aren't enrolled. Check if your parents or grandparents qualify.

walmart_4_dollar.name: Walmart $4 Generic Program
walmart_4_dollar.howItWorks: Over 100 common generics for $4/30-day or $10/90-day supply.
walmart_4_dollar.tip: Some of the most common medications are on this list: metformin, lisinopril, amlodipine, omeprazole.

generalTips: Always ask: 'Is there a generic version?' — generics are FDA-required to be equally effective and cost 80-90% less
90-day mail-order prescriptions are 20-40% cheaper than 30-day pharmacy fills
Compare prices at 3-4 pharmacies — prices vary wildly for the same drug (Costco is often cheapest)
Costco pharmacy does NOT require a Costco membership — anyone can use it
If your doctor prescribes a brand-name drug, ask if a generic or therapeutic alternative exists
Use your FSA/HSA for prescription costs — pre-tax dollars save an additional 22-37%
Split pills: some drugs cost the same regardless of dose. A 20mg pill split in half = two 10mg doses at half price. Ask your pharmacist if this is safe for your medication.
Prescription discount cards (GoodRx, SingleCare, RxSaver) are free and often beat insurance copays
